articles.title: How rescue of US airman in remote part of Iran unfolded
articles.description: The operation to extract him from the ground in hostile territory was hugely complex and involved multiple US government agencies.

articles.title: Analysis: Trump declares victory in Iran war after rescue, but threats to US operation still loom
articles.description: The rescue could impact how Trump views a ground operation to take Kharg Island or to seize enriched uranium sites.

articles.title: Hungary alleges plot to blow up gas pipeline ahead of election
articles.description: The incident comes a week before the polls, and follow warnings of a potential operations staged to influence voters.

articles.title: Pope Leo calls for global leaders to choose peace in his first Easter Mass
articles.description: Pope Leo XIV addressed thousands of worshippers gathered in St Peter's Square on Easter Sunday in his first address as pontiff.

articles.title: Cambodia unveils statue to honour famous landmine-sniffing rat
articles.description: The statue honours the late African giant pouched rat who sniffed out more than 100 landmines during his lifetime.

articles.title: Artemis's stunning Moon pictures - science or holiday photos?
articles.description: The story behind the beautiful pictures beamed back to Earth from the Artemis II astronauts.

articles.title: AI videos fuel rhetoric as Orbán bids for four more years in Hungary
articles.description: Videos have targeted Viktor Orbán's election rival, who could unseat him after 16 years in office.

articles.title: Man charged over fatal shooting of baby in pram in New York
articles.description: Two men have been arrested over the shooting of a seven-month-old baby in Williamsburg.

articles.title: Unanswered questions remain after Australia's most wanted fugitive killed in standoff
articles.description: Double-murderer Dezi Freeman evaded capture for seven months in the bush but police believe he had help.

articles.title: How Bombay went from a fort city to a bustling metropolis
articles.description: A new exhibition charts Bombay's passage through the centuries via paintings, photographs and prints.

articles.title: Myanmar's coup leader who set off a brutal civil war becomes president
articles.description: General Min Aung Hlaing has been chosen as the next president by the newly-elected parliament.

articles.title: Fears cost of water and beer to soar as India's scorching summer hits
articles.description: Makers of plastic and glass bottles are struggling to access raw materials as the war squeezes supplies.

articles.title: Iconic 19th Century painting sets Indian art record with $17.9m sale
articles.description: Yashoda and Krishna, painted by Raja Ravi Varma, beat a record set by an MF Husain work last year.

articles.title: Magnitude 7.4 earthquake hits off Indonesia, killing one
articles.description: The quake struck between the islands of Manado and Ternate. Tsunami warnings have now been lifted.

articles.title: China is trying to play peacemaker in the Iran war - will it work?
articles.description: It's a turnaround for Beijing, whose official response has been muted so far. Why is China stepping in now?

articles.title: Divorced couples in Japan can now share custody of their children
articles.description: Japan was, until recently, the only G7 country to not recognise the legal concept of joint custody.

articles.title: Pepsi withdraws as UK festival sponsor after Kanye West backlash
articles.description: Sir Keir Starmer says it is "deeply concerning" the rapper is set to headline a festival after recent antisemitic comments.

articles.title: Royals attend Windsor Easter Sunday service
articles.description: Andrew-Mountbatten Windsor and his family are absent from the traditional family event.

articles.title: Archbishop prays for Middle East peace in first Easter sermon
articles.description: Dame Sarah Mullally called for an 'end to the violence and destruction' before a congregation in Canterbury.

articles.title: Families homeless as tree hits houses in Storm Dave
articles.description: As Storm Dave brought heavy winds to the country, a tree fell into two homes in Kinver.

articles.title: Comedians tell ministers lack of funding is no laughing matter
articles.description: Culture Minister Ian Murray has agreed to work with the comedy industry to boost support after talks.

articles.title: Man charged with riotous behaviour after football match disorder
articles.description: The disorder, which happened before the Irish Cup semi-final at Windsor Park, saw fireworks, flares and bottles thrown.

articles.title: Water supply issues resolved after three days
articles.description: South East Water apologised and said it was continuing to release isolated air pockets in the system.

articles.title: Family 'utterly devastated' after boy, 13, killed in crash as two charged
articles.description: Noah Campbell's family say he was a "talented, versatile sportsman" who was "incredibly well-liked".
